workItem.title: Bound Codex event memory and enforce daemon resource recovery

## Context

A real three-agent recovery drill found the Symphony BEAM process at 82% CPU and a 5.5 GB memory peak after roughly 53 minutes. The dashboard stopped responding. Restart recovery succeeded without duplicate worktrees, but unbounded in-memory Codex payload retention and missing service resource limits can exhaust X1 again.

## Expected outcome

Symphony stores only bounded summaries of Codex events, tests prove large payloads are not retained, and the X1 service has a recoverable memory ceiling so it restarts and resumes canonical AgentRuns before host stability is endangered.

## Scope

- Bound last Codex event/message data retained by Orchestrator
- Add regression tests using multi-megabyte event payloads
- Add observable memory/restart evidence
- Install systemd memory ceiling and graceful restart policy
- Re-run restart recovery without duplicate workspaces or AgentRuns

## Out of scope

- Reducing the number of concurrent agents below the configured policy
- Discarding durable audit evidence

## Acceptance criteria

- Large Codex payloads do not remain in Orchestrator state
- Dashboard remains responsive with bounded summaries
- Full Elixir gate and coverage pass
- Service memory is capped by systemd with automatic recovery
- Restart resumes existing runs and creates no duplicate worktrees
